requirement: Buyer can initiate supplier onboarding process by providing basic supplier information.
Supplier onboarding process undergoes internal approval workflow before sending invitation to supplier.
Supplier receives invitation via email to register with AECI and complete due diligence steps.
Supplier information is accurate and current, and certifications and contact information are visible.
Multiple channels of managing supplier information are eliminated, and supplier participation is facilitated.
Supplier is created in Coupa only after completing the onboarding process.
Supplier status management includes purchase block, payment block, supplier deactivation, and reactivation.
Approval workflows involve Procurement, Finance, General Accounting, and Master Data teams.
Integration touchpoints exist between Coupa SIM and SAP for supplier creation, updates, and activation.
Internal users can register suppliers in Coupa for urgent or business-critical situations, adhering to governance and approval controls.
Buyers can update vendor information in Coupa, including status changes.
The One-Time Vendor process enables faster vendor creation with a simplified approval flow and reduced data requirements, while ensuring basic compliance and procurement controls.
Coupa displays supplier status labels to help users quickly see the status of each supplier.
The system accommodates geography-specific requirements, including field-level requirements, attachments, and language support.
